title: Press & Scratch Test

placement.where: Chapter 6 'Materials Around Us' · 6.3.2 Which materials are hard? (Activity 6.5). Students press and scratch each object to test it, then record whether it is hard or soft.

windowMethods: press(id|array) — press object(s) to test softness/squishiness (soft ones visibly squish); id is 'mattress'|'sweater'|'soft_toy'|'steel_tumbler'|'wooden_table'|'brick' or an array.
scratch(id|array) — scratch object(s) to test hardness (soft ones get a mark); id is 'mattress'|'sweater'|'soft_toy'|'steel_tumbler'|'wooden_table'|'brick' or an array.

studentControls.control: Press an object (tap the object emoji)
studentControls.effect: the object squishes (soft) or keeps its shape (hard); the orange squish-meter fills to its softness; its material and a HARD/SOFT tag reveal

studentControls.control: Scratch an object (the 'scratch' button under each object)
studentControls.effect: a soft object picks up a visible red scratch mark; a hard object resists; reveals its material/tag

studentControls.control: Record Hard / Soft per object in the table
studentControls.effect: a correct tap locks in with a green glow and ✓; a wrong tap shakes the row, increments retries, and gives a hint
clears all presses, scratches and recorded decisions and reshuffles the table order

answerKey.rule: An object keeps its shape when pressed if it is hard, and changes shape if it is soft. A scratch test agrees: a softer surface is marked more easily. Whether an object is hard or soft is decided by the material it is made of (NCERT Class 6, Chapter 6, p.107).
answerKey.verbNotes: press(id|array) and scratch(id|array) run the physical test; select(id|array) / tap(id|array) records the table decision. select accepts a bare id (uses the correct hardness) or {id, choice:'hard'|'soft'}. All 'do' verbs accept a single id OR an array to credit several objects at once.
answerKey.operatorModeNote: ai = focused demo: the press/scratch phenomenon and the read-out stay visible, but the per-object scratch buttons, the Record Hard/Soft taps and the bottom action bar go inert (the agent drives via press()/scratch()/select()/highlight()); a '👩‍🏫 watch' caption + chip show. student = full interactive controls. Default student.

scientificFacts: Objects are made of materials, and the material decides whether an object is hard or soft.
A hard object (steel, wood, baked clay) keeps its shape when you press it.
A soft object (foam, wool, cotton) changes its shape easily when pressed and is scratched more easily.
Hardness is a property of the material, not of the object's size or weight.
